medicalReport:
PATIENT PRESENTATION:
Chief Complaint: Persistent migraines with visual disturbances
Symptoms: Recurring headaches 3-4 times per week, photophobia, nausea, blurred vision, neck stiffness
Duration: 6 months, progressively worsening

MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Hypertension (8 years)
- Type 2 Diabetes (5 years)
- Hypothyroidism (12 years)

CURRENT MEDICATIONS:
1. Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
2. Metformin 1000mg twice daily
3. Levothyroxine 75mcg once daily

ALLERGIES:
- Sulfonamides (severe rash)
- Codeine (nausea)
- Penicillin (anaphylaxis risk)
